name: Cage the Elephant
description: Cage the Elephant is an American rock band formed in Bowling Green, Kentucky in 2006 by brothers Matt and Brad Shultz. Known for their genre-bending sound and explosive live performances, they have won two Grammy Awards for Best Rock Album and scored 13 number-one hits on the Billboard Alternative Airplay chart.
trivia: The band's name came from a mentally disabled man who approached them after a show in 2006, repeatedly saying 'You have to cage the elephant.'
Their song 'Ain't No Rest for the Wicked' was featured as the opening theme of the video game Borderlands and is certified 8x Platinum by the RIAA.
When drummer Jared Champion's appendix burst during a 2011 tour with Foo Fighters, Dave Grohl himself filled in on drums.
'Cigarette Daydreams' became their first song to reach 1 billion streams on Spotify in late 2024.
The band has tied Green Day and Linkin Park for second-most number-one hits on the Billboard Alternative Airplay chart with 13.
Lead guitarist Lincoln Parish was only 16 when the band moved to London; his parents had to sign over parental guardianship to the band.
